column1: # against the `pulldown-latex` parser Event stream. See README.md.
#   token        the TeX symbol AS WRITTEN; leading `\` => command row, else
#                (Ord | Bin | Rel | Open | Close | Punct | Op).
#                `Ord` on a command row means "not in the table" (command_class
#                returns None; the formatter defaults such commands to Ord).
#   oracle       expected pulldown-latex Content variant for the probe `a <tok> b`
#                (binop | relation | largeop | function | open | close | punct |
#                ordinary), or `skip` when the symbol emits no probeable Content
# Lines beginning with `#` and blank lines are ignored.
# DIVERGENCE: AMS makes \asymp a relation (\mathrel); pulldown-latex emits it as
# a binary op. We keep Rel (correct) and record the oracle quirk here so a
# future pulldown bump that "fixes" it surfaces as a fixture review, not a
# DIVERGENCE: pulldown-latex classifies \lim as a Function, not a large
# operator. For spacing both are Op-like (Ord-like, and coerce a following
# +/- to unary), so we keep Op and record the oracle's Function here.
